sglang.title: Support Online Quantization for W8A8
sglang.author: hebiao064
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Closes: #4434
We found that online quantization of w8a8 is not supported, #4434 directly load a fp16 model with --quantization w8a8_fp8 will load a model with all weights as zero hence the benchmark result is nearly 0 for GSM8K.
This PR added such support and showcased reasonable benchma

sglang.title: Nicer standalone engine inferface
sglang.author: yinghai
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Two improvement

Make Engine a context manager so that we don't forget to release the resource (e.g. dev memory) when we are done.
Make it optional to send SIGKILL to parent process when engine quit unexpectedly so that we can actually catch the error in pytest process.

Modifications

Ch

sglang.title: [Fix] Resolve GPU Memory Leak in update_weights_from_tensor
sglang.author: U-rara
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
My mentor @fukun07 and I discovered a significant CUDA memory leak in the update_weights_from_tensor functionality when tensor parallelism (TP) > 1. The input CUDA tensors used for updates fail to properly release their GPU memory after their lifecycle completes.
The root cause relates to

sglang.author: yinghai
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Improvement of sgl-router to avoid hang.
Modifications

Fix sgl-router side of http payload handling to drain the payload when
we hit early error. This covers the scenario of 404, and 413 large
payload for json scenario.
Align sglang client/server keepalive expectations to avoid sgl-route

sglang.title: Revert "feat: update grouped_topk to support softmax and sigmoid"
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Reverts #3680
Related to issue #4497.
Currently grouped_topk is only used for DeepSeek-V2/V2.5, biased_grouped_topk is used for DeepSeek-V3/R1. For DeepSeek-V2, the scoring function is always softmax, so don't need to add new option.

vllm.title: setup.py: drop assumption about local `main` branch
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
PR #13921 added an assumption about the local main branch. We're
already getting the latest commit ref for main upstream, so using this
branch is not necessary in the rest of the logic. What we really care
about is upstream main and the git branch we're currently on.
This change should keep the same

vllm.title: [MISC] More AMD unused var clean up
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Will force the -Wno-unused-variable for AMD build in the following PR.
Maybe just delete them in the future build. This is non-intrusive change to be safe.
cc: @hongxiayang

vllm.title: fix minor miscalled method
vllm.author: kushanam

vllm.title: [V1][TPU] Apply the ragged paged attention kernel fix and remove the padding.
vllm.author: vanbasten23
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In #14597, we added the padding due to an ragged paged attention kernel issue. Since we have fixed the issue in the kernel, we can remove the padding in vLLM. This will make the kernel run more efficiently.
Test plan:

Repro script for #14597
VLLM_USE_V1=1 pytest -s -v vllm/tests/entrypoints/llm/tes

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix Ultravox on V1
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #13161 updated _process_audio_input to return one tensor per audio chunk, which broke the model on V1.

vllm.title: [Misc] Add `--seed` option to offline multi-modal examples
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Similar to #14893, but now for the example scripts.
Note: I decided to keep the default seed to None since these examples are user-facing. To check the model implementation, we can simply append --seed 0 to the command.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Model] Mixtral: use unused head_dim config argument
vllm.author: qtrrb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
MixtralConfig has a a head_dim argument that will never be used as MixtralAttention always uses hidden_size // self.total_num_heads as its value.
This change brings MixtralAttention closer to LlamaAttention, passing the config in order to check for the attribute head_dim and using the value if it is

Ok, well I ended up doing a few things in this PR:

upgrade xgrammar to 0.1.16 to add support for vision models in v1.
moving/simplify a lot of the metadata logics from our side and use xgrammar API for this construction

Reasoning:

xgrammar updated some core API, which vLLM was depending on in pre

This PR fixes the CI/CD runner to run the following tests (that currently work):

    && VLLM_USE_V1=1 pytest -v -s /workspace/vllm/tests/v1/tpu/test_basic.py \

    && VLLM_USE_V1=1 pytest -v -s /workspace/vllm/tests/entrypoints/llm/test_accuracy.py::test_lm_

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix bnb quantization for models with both HF-format and Mistral-format weights
vllm.author: tristanleclercq
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the inflight quantization with bitsandbytes for models that have both HF-format weights (model*.safetensors) and Mistral-format (consolidated*.safetensors) weights. The issue was observed for instance with:

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3
mistralai/Mistral-Nemo-Instruct-2407

As ex
